note: Each category carries TWO prompt sets. `violation` = the policy breach itself (human nudity/explicit, REAL weapons, illegal drugs and paraphernalia). `review` = the look-alike a human should judge (swimwear/lingerie, toy or replica weapons, tobacco/vape). `negatives` are subtracted from BOTH and exist to kill the true false-positive class: mannequins, statues, sculptures and other non-person figures never flag. Tracks are PROMPT SETS, not tag lookups — the 2177-tag vocabulary has no nudity concept at all, only clothing, and a swimsuit is not nudity.

nudity.label: nudity / sexual content
nudity.note: TWO INSTRUMENTS by design (research/track-nudity.md, measured 2026-07-22). VIOLATION = the Marqo NSFW head (pixels, dedicated 5.6M-param model), benchmark-cited 98.56%/20k, and NOT locally re-measurable under the EVAL DATA LAW. REVIEW = a prompt-ensemble MARGIN over the index embeddings (`review_subcategories` below), because the Marqo head scores swimwear in the SAME 0.05-0.15 band as mannequins and cannot carry the review boundary (swimwear p50 0.056 == mannequin p50 0.056, measured). The `violation` prompt list here is the offline zero-shot FALLBACK vocabulary, never the review instrument. `negatives` are subtracted from the review margin and deliberately include the ADR-14 never-flag figures (mannequin/statue) so `control_never_flag` stays below the FP band by construction.
review: a person in a bikini
a person in swimwear at the beach
a woman in a bikini
a man in swim trunks
a person in a swimsuit
a person in lingerie
a woman in lingerie
a person wearing a bra and underwear
a person in their underwear
a person in briefs
a person in boxer shorts
a shirtless man
a bare-chested man
a muscular man with no shirt

swimwear: a person in swimwear at the beach
lingerie: a person wearing a bra and underwear
bare-chest-male: a muscular man with no shirt
artistic-figure: an artistic figure study of a body
a fine-art photograph of a bare human form
medical-context: a clinical medical photograph of a patient's skin
a dermatology photo of bare skin

negatives: a portrait of a person wearing clothes
an everyday object on a table
a mannequin in a shop window
a marble statue of a human figure
a sculpture of a human body

fit.review_feature: margin = max(review concepts) - max(negatives incl. mannequin/statue)
fit.probe: data/nudity-probe — 202 local Unsplash review-tier TPs by KEYWORD-JOIN (T4, no agent hand-labelling): swimwear 77 / lingerie 16 / underwear 4 / bare-chest-male 45 (noisy `fitness` proxy) / mannequin-statue control 60
fit.fp_band: 14,734 indexed safe images (COCO val2017 + Unsplash), probe photo-ids excluded from the negative pool

lingerie.verdict: DOES NOT SEPARATE on shared embedding — distilled head owed (TRACKS T2)

underwear.verdict: DOES NOT SEPARATE (also n=4, too thin)

fit.violation: Marqo head, argmax tau_violation=0.50, published 98.56%/20k — NOT locally reproduced (EVAL DATA LAW). Honest margin: the safe-corpus Marqo p99 is 0.175, so 0.50 sits far above the FP band.
fit.review: swimwear SEPARATES (R@5%=0.57 recall-first); lingerie/underwear/bare-chest DO NOT separate on the shared pecore embedding (R@5% <= 0.13). Review recall is swimwear-only until a distilled head is trained.
fit.caveat: Small positive sets (swimwear 77, lingerie 16, underwear 4) => wide CI. The Marqo head CANNOT serve the review tier (swimwear p50 0.056 == mannequin p50 0.056). tau_review is a recall-first review-queue threshold, never an enforcement decision (enforcement_ready stays false).

bows_crossbows: a person aiming a bow and arrow

weapons.note: Enforcement ships via the trained head (weapons-<backend>.json); these prompt sets are the zero-shot fallback + taxonomy record. Toy/replica = review (ADR-14); real weapons = violation. GAPS (no held-out OI imagery): explosives_grenades (0 `Bomb` images), submachine/automatic + crossbow have no distinct OI label so are unmeasured separately.

fit.version: v2 (2026-07-22) — refit after b-daemon/b-app reported 4 measured defects
fit.corpus: 15,010 real-photo negatives (COCO val2017 + Unsplash demo + Unsplash-b, deduped by photo id) — ~2x b-app's 7,790, the corpus that saturated v1.
fit.violation: AP 0.472 · recall .882 at 1% FP · fitted on 17 hand-verified drug images. At the shipped tau: 88% drug recall as violation, 94% surfaced (viol+review), 1.06% of negatives flagged violation — matches the fit's own FP prediction.
fit.p_distribution: SPREAD, not saturated: 0 negatives at p>=0.9, 1 in [0.7,0.9] (the b-app '218 at p=0.99' bug is structurally gone — evidence cap P_MAX + gentle ridge slope). Full histogram in research/eval-drugs.json.
fit.review_band: tau_review 0.0083 < tau 0.0100 — a band BELOW violation (fixes b-daemon defect #1). Tobacco/vape routed here by the arbitration; vape acceptance PASS.
fit.defect_3_leaf: the raspberry/bramble-leaf p=0.92 FP had TWO causes, both fixed: (a) it was MISLABELLED as a cannabis positive in my ground truth — removed after a full-res re-audit of all 18; (b) serrated/compound-leaf negatives added.
fit.caveat: 17 drug positives — a wide-CI estimate, not a benchmark. Recall on cocaine/heroin/meth imagery specifically is UNMEASURED (no labelled image exists in any corpus we may use). Tobacco review-tier recall stays weak (small-object ceiling).

sports.note: CONTENT track, not moderation (ADR-14 `match` tier, 13:23Z) — routed to the `content` bucket via content_track:true, never summed into violation/review. `match_labels` is parallel to `match` (same order); the argmax match concept's label rides on the flag as `label`. NEGATIVES ARE GENERIC-ONLY BY DESIGN — measured: scene hard-negatives (empty stadium, snowy mountain, beach) drop COCO AP 0.932->0.904 and recall@prec0.80 0.947->0.889 because they sit next to real sports scenes and steal the max-background term. Do NOT add hard negatives without re-measuring (research/track-sports.md §3). The 7 borderline sports (chess/hiking/fishing/darts-pool/esports/yoga/dance) are FOLDED INTO negatives so they read as `none` by default (measured: unsplash hiking 0.213 out-of-bank -> 0.000 in-bank); a site that wants them counted removes them from negatives and re-scores one sidecar column (TRACKS.md T3). τ_match lives in the per-model fitted file data/moderation/sports-<model_id>.json, never here.

note_drugs: drugs: `policy_neighbours` are scored for EXPLANATION ONLY and must never be subtracted — measured, subtracting them collapses AP 0.58 to 0.04 (a clinical syringe is visually identical to a drug syringe). See research/track-drugs.md.
note_tiers: Tier vocabulary is DATA: a category's prompt-set keys are its tiers, ordered alert > violation > review (moderation) and `match` (content, never counted as moderation). Adding a track = adding a spec entry; the reader needs no change. Owning lanes add their own categories.
